rows.description: Credlington Augm. red ad Fest. Purificatiōs
rows.notes: Page heading across the top. Ink and abbreviation make exact Latin ending uncertain (appears as 'Purificatiōs'); no amounts in this line and heading is clearly separate from monetary columns.

Pounds/shillings/pence shown clearly as '18.0.0' to the left of the description. Description handwriting for the name is slightly unclear ('Sre' vs 'Ste'/'Mr'), so confidence <1.0 for the text but numeric columns are unambiguous and valid.
Clear numeric '2:8:6' immediately left of the description; description 'Morton vicar. pro Triennio' is legible. All currency fields within valid ranges.

Right-hand monetary columns aligned to this row read '17 13 4'. The description is mostly legible though the first word could be read a couple of ways ('Ladlane' vs 'Ladlane/Ladlané'); numeric alignment with vertical ruling is clear so amounts are reliable.

Monetary trio '06 13 4' is clearly aligned with this description. 'Medenham St: Thomas' reading is clear though the parish name could be spelled slightly differently in other sources; currency digits are unambiguous and valid.

Indented, braced sub-item under previous rows (brace visible). No monetary figures written on this line. The lead abbreviation before 'Backgate' ('te') is faint/uncertain and name 'George' is legible; treated as an entry with no amount.
Second name under the same brace (appears as 'Jennins'). No amounts on this line. Name legible though spelling could vary (Jenning(s)/Jennins); treated as an entry/sub-item.
Underlined sum in the right-hand columns '102 17 0' corresponding to the group above. No descriptive text to left; alignment and digits are clear and within valid ranges.
Centered section header. No monetary amounts on the header line; text is legible.

rows.description: Reed of mr Hutchins senr. Bursar for increments
rows.notes: Right-hand columns read '20. 3. 4' with a small loop/mark after the pence interpreted as 'ob' (halfpenny). The wording 'Reed of mr Hutchins senr. Bursar for increments' is legible; fraction recognition is slightly uncertain but the loop is consistent with a halfpenny mark.
Centered sub-section header 'Gent. Coroners'. Clear and distinct with no amounts on header line.
Right-hand amount '16 00 0' aligned with this line. The names/abbreviations 'mr Hra. Hoblyn' and 'Tremain' are legible though abbreviated; numeric columns are clear and valid.
Right-hand amount '16 00 0' clearly aligned. Description 'mr. Jo. Bassett & mr. Yeo 8t' readable though abbreviated; all numeric fields clear.

Name line 'Mrs Art Supper' (or similar) appears with no amount beside it. Handwriting is somewhat faint/abbreviated; no monetary columns aligned to this specific line, so recorded as an entry without amount.

rows.description: mr. Baron, Bradford, Carshlake, Lome, White
rows.notes: Right-hand amount '10 00 0' aligned with this multi-name entry. The list of surnames is legible though some place/surname spellings (Carshlake/Larkshlake etc.) may vary; currency digits are clear.
Right-hand monetary trio reads '06 1 0' which corresponds to £0 6s 1d; alignment with description is clear. Description text is legible; numeric ranges valid.

rows.description: Recd by bill of Exch. from my ld. ArchBp. of Armagh to provide a frame for his Picture
rows.notes: Long description is legible though abbreviated in places ('Recd by bill of Exch. from my ld. ArchBp. of Armagh'); right-hand amount '10 00 0' is clearly written and aligned to this line.
Underlined final sum for the Coroners section reads '78 4 4' in the right-hand columns. Digits and alignment are clear; no descriptive text on this line.
